title: anacardiaceae
intro: The Anacardiaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the cashew or sumac family. This family consists of approximately 70 genera and over 600 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Anacardiaceae plants are characterized by their small, often fragrant flowers and their production of nuts, seeds, or other edible fruits. Examples of plants within this family include the cashew tree (Anacardium occidentale), poison ivy (Toxicodendron radicans), and the pistachio tree (Pistacia vera).
